stem: A ski jump ramp rises 15 feet above the ground. The ramp makes a 30-degree angle with the horizontal. A rubber mat that is 6 feet wide covers the entire surface of the ramp. The ski resort is open 120 days per year. What is the area of the rubber mat, in square feet?
explanation: The number of open days (120) is extra information that should be ignored. The ramp forms the hypotenuse of a right triangle with a vertical rise of 15 feet and an angle of 30 degrees with the horizontal. Using sin(30) = opposite/hypotenuse: 1/2 = 15/ramp, so ramp = 30 feet. The rubber mat area is 30 * 6 = 180 square feet.

distractorRationale.A: Used the height of the ramp (15 feet) as the ramp length without applying trigonometry: area = 15 * 6 = 90
distractorRationale.C: Used cos(30) instead of sin(30) to find the ramp length: ramp = 15/cos(30) = 15/(sqrt(3)/2) = 10*sqrt(3), area = 10*sqrt(3) * 6 = 60\sqrt{3}
distractorRationale.D: Set up the trig ratio inverted, multiplying instead of dividing: ramp = 15 * sin(30) = 15 * (1/2) = 7.5, area = 7.5 * 6 = 45

imageDescription: A right triangle with the vertical side on the left labeled 15 ft (height of the ramp), the horizontal base at the bottom (ground level), and the hypotenuse representing the ramp surface. The angle between the ramp and the horizontal ground is labeled 30 degrees. The ramp width (6 ft) is indicated along the surface of the hypotenuse.
